prs.title: fix: #4298 respect prefers-reduced-motion for loading indicators
prs.body:
### Summary

- Replace animated inline SVG **mask-image** values in loading components with static SVGs when **prefers-reduced-motion: reduce** is active. This removes motion while keeping the same visual appearance.

### Changes

- Update only: **packages/daisyui/src/components/loading.css**
- Add a **@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)** override that swaps animated masks with static equivalents.

### Testing

- Run **bun run build**
- In DevTools → Rendering → emulate **prefers-reduced-motion: reduce**
- Confirm all loading variants (spinner, dots, ring, ball, bars, infinity) are rendered without animation.

### Notes

- CSS-only change.
- No class name or API changes.
- Scope limited to loading indicators only, per maintainer feedback.
- No modifications to collapse, dropdown, carousel, or modal.

prs.title: Implement Z-Algorithm in Swift
prs.body: This pull request adds an implementation of the Z-Algorithm in Swift. The Z-Algorithm computes the Z-array for a given string, where each element Z[i] represents the length of the longest substring starting at position i that matches the prefix of the string. This is useful for efficient string pattern matching and other string-related algorithms. The implementation includes a function zAlgorithm(for:) that takes a string and returns the Z-array, along with an example usage demonstrating the algorithm on the string "aabcaabxaaz"

prs.title: feat(leaderboard): Implement timing-based sorting and display
prs.body:
fix:https://github.com/nst-sdc/QuickSnatch-Platform/issues/2

- Add submission_time and qr_scan_time fields to User model
- Update leaderboard to sort by first scan time
- Display flag submission, QR scan, and first scan timestamps
- Add visual styling for different timing types
- Implement database migration for new timing fields

This commit improves the leaderboard by:
- Tracking when users submit flags and scan QR codes
- Sorting entries based on first scan order
- Providing clear visual distinction between different timing types
- Maintaining accurate timing records for competition fairness

A technical comment, you are free to remove or leave it as it is when PR is created

utils/changelog/changelog.py
tests/ci/cancel_and_rerun_workflow_lambda/app.py
-->
### Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature
- Experimental Feature
- Improvement
- Performance Improvement
- Backward Incompatible Change
- Build/Testing/Packaging Improvement
- Documentation (changelog entry is not required)
- Critical Bug Fix (crash, LOGICAL_ERROR, data loss, RBAC)
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)
- Not for changelog (changelog entry is not required)


### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):
...

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

<!---
Directly edit documentation source files in the "docs" folder with the same pull-request as code changes

or

Add a user-readable short description of the changes that should be added to docs.clickhouse.com below.

At a minimum, the following information should be added (but add more as needed).
- Motivation: Why is this function, table engine, etc. useful to ClickHouse users?

- Parameters: If the feature being added takes arguments, options or is influenced by settings, please list them below with a brief explanation.

- Example use: A query or command.
-->

issues.title: bug: A11y: Components ignore prefers-reduced-motion (carousel, loading, collapse, dropdown)
issues.body:
### Reproduction URL (Required)

https://play.tailwindcss.com/7PTqWz1XeD

### What version of daisyUI are you using?

5.5.5

### Which browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### Describe your issue

**Expected**

When OS or browser requests reduced motion (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce):

- Loading indicators render a static visual (no movement).
- Collapse opens/closes without animated transitions.
- Dropdown opens/closes without fade/scale animation.
- Carousel does not smooth-scroll; snap still works.

**Actual**

- Loading animations continue to move (animated SVG masks).
- Collapse and dropdown animate transitions.
- Carousel uses smooth scrolling.
- This creates unnecessary motion for users who requested reduced motion.

**Notes**

- This seems component CSS-related (independent of framework/build tools).
- I can implement a minimal, CSS-only fix via targeted @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides.

issues.title: [BUG] The Important Headings are Not Visible
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The footer headings ("Math 4 Python," "Quick Links," "Follow Us") are not visible due to missing or incorrect CSS styles, likely caused by the text color blending with the background.

**How are you going to fix it?**
I will fix the issue by adding or correcting the CSS styles for the footer headings. Specifically, I’ll ensure that the text color of the headings is set to a visible color (e.g., white or another contrasting color) so that it doesn't blend with the background.
